Flex Platform

Get more from
your energy system.

Flex gives you complete visibility and control of your energy systems — helping you reduce costs, improve performance, and maximise value.

Most energy systems are installed and then left to run — with limited visibility into how they're actually performing and no easy way to act on what the data is telling you.

Flex changes that. Nine dedicated modules each answer a specific question, giving you the tools to understand, control, and optimise your system at every level.

01 — Flex:Assure

Is my system delivering what was promised?

Know your system is performing as designed.

Flex:Assure gives you continuous validation that your system is performing as originally specified. It compares real-world generation and performance against design-stage forecasts, and contextualises any shortfall — whether the cause is weather, operating conditions, or a genuine system issue. You get ongoing confidence, not just at installation, but every day after.

Actual vs expected ForecastFit indicators Performance deviation Weather context

Commercial Value

Eliminate ambiguity around system performance. Get verifiable evidence that your investment is delivering — and the early warning you need when it isn't.

Why it matters

Without benchmarking, underperformance goes undetected. Flex:Assure gives you the ongoing confidence that your system is working as designed — and the early warning you need when it isn't.

Example

Solar generation is down 18% on forecast. Flex:Assure attributes the shortfall to recorded cloud cover for the period — no fault found. Your team moves on, and you can see exactly why.

02 — Flex:Insights

In Development

What operational improvements should I make?

Turn energy data into decisions that save money.

Flex:Insights analyses how your site actually behaves — consumption patterns, solar generation, import and export flows, battery use — and translates that into clear, actionable recommendations. Not generic advice. Specific, financially quantified changes grounded in your real data. It becomes a continuous improvement engine, surfacing new opportunities as your operation evolves.

Load pattern analysis Solar self-consumption Financial quantification Continuous improvement

Commercial Value

You shouldn't need to be an energy analyst to improve your system's performance. Flex:Insights does the heavy lifting — consistently surfacing the changes with the greatest financial upside, in plain language.

Why it matters

Energy data on its own has limited value. Flex:Insights bridges the gap between charts and action — ensuring the value hidden in your data is converted into decisions you can actually use.

Example

Hot water heating runs before solar peaks each morning. Shifting the load two hours later increases solar self-consumption by 22% and reduces grid imports during off-solar windows — with a dollar value attached.

Flex:Insights — Recommendations

03 — Flex:Tariff

In Development

Am I on the right pricing structure?

Match your tariff to how you actually use energy.

Your electricity tariff was probably chosen based on assumptions that no longer reflect how you operate. Flex:Tariff uses your real interval consumption data to compare every available retail tariff against your actual usage profile — including custom commercial offers not publicly listed. The result is a clear, evidence-based answer to a question that's costing you money every month.

Interval data analysis Tariff catalogue Custom commercial offers Financial ranking

Commercial Value

Switching to the right tariff can unlock significant savings without changing any hardware. It's one of the simplest improvements you can make — and Flex:Tariff gives you the evidence to do it with confidence.

Why it matters

You may already be on the wrong tariff — especially if your operating hours, solar system, or usage patterns have changed since it was set. Flex:Tariff ensures your commercial structure is as well-matched to your site as the hardware itself.

Example

A commercial site on a legacy flat tariff is compared against 12 plans. A time-of-use tariff, combined with a custom retailer offer, would have saved $4,200 over the previous 12 months based on actual usage.

04 — Flex:Simulator

In Development

What would have happened if I operated differently?

Test strategies on real data. Before you commit.

Flex:Simulator takes your actual historical site data and replays it under alternative operating scenarios. Want to know what would happen if the battery charged later, reserved capacity for peak pricing, or exported differently? You'll see the real financial outcome — grounded in how your system actually ran, not a theoretical model. Know what a change is worth before you make it.

Historical backtesting Scenario modelling Financial comparison Side-by-side analysis

Commercial Value

Stop guessing. Flex:Simulator lets you identify missed value and validate new strategies before making any changes to live operations — so when you do act, you do it with evidence, not assumptions.

Why it matters

Without backtesting, optimisation is guesswork. Flex:Simulator lets you quantify the impact of any change before you implement it — so you move forward with confidence, not risk.

Example

You replay the previous month with a revised battery strategy — charging later in the solar window, discharging into peak pricing periods. The simulation shows $1,100 in additional monthly value. You haven't changed a setting yet.

05 — Flex:Schedule

Give me simple control over how the system runs.

Simple, rule-based control that works from day one.

Not every site needs sophisticated automation. Flex:Schedule lets you define exactly when your battery charges and discharges using simple, time-based rules. Align operation with your solar generation windows, peak tariff periods, or business hours — no technical expertise required. And when you're ready to go further, it's the natural foundation for simulation and full optimisation.

Time-based scheduling Battery charge/discharge Tariff window alignment Export control

Commercial Value

A simple, well-configured schedule consistently outperforms default or unmanaged battery behaviour. You get immediate improvement from day one — and a clear path toward full optimisation when you want it.

Why it matters

Most systems run on default settings that don't account for your actual site conditions or tariff structure. Flex:Schedule is the simplest, most immediate way to align battery operation with how you actually use energy.

Example

A business on a time-of-use tariff configures the battery to charge between 10am–2pm (peak solar) and discharge between 5pm–8pm (peak pricing). Evening grid costs reduce significantly with no ongoing manual intervention.

06 — Flex:Optimisation

In Development

Automatically operate the system for the best outcome.

Automated energy management. The next step.

Flex:Optimisation is the natural evolution beyond scheduling. Instead of fixed rules, it uses forecasts of solar generation, your consumption, and electricity pricing to dynamically determine the optimal way to run your energy assets — every hour, every day. Your system continuously adapts to real conditions, so you capture more value without lifting a finger.

Dynamic control Forecast-driven Automated operation Real-time adaptation

Commercial Value

Static schedules leave value on the table. Optimisation ensures your system responds to what's actually happening — not a plan set weeks ago. It's the highest-value outcome from the Flex platform, unlocking continuous improvement without ongoing manual effort from you.

Why it matters

A dynamic energy environment requires a dynamic response. Optimisation delivers the maximum financial return from your infrastructure without ongoing manual involvement — freeing you to focus on what matters most.

Example

On a forecast of high afternoon solar and elevated evening pricing, the system automatically delays the morning charge, extends the discharge window, and maximises export during peak price periods — all without manual input.

Flex:Optimisation — Dynamic Dispatch

07 — Flex:Submetering

Show me where energy is being used across the site.

Visibility into every corner of your site's energy use.

Whole-of-site data tells you the total, but not the story behind it. Flex:Submetering monitors individual circuits, buildings, and assets across your site, so you can see exactly what's driving your consumption. Compare usage across different areas, identify where energy is being wasted, and allocate costs accurately — with real data, not estimates.

Multi-point monitoring Circuit-level tracking Usage breakdown Cost allocation

Commercial Value

You can't manage what you can't see. Flex:Submetering turns high-level totals into operational intelligence — giving you the visibility to identify waste, justify investment, and act where it matters most.

Why it matters

Your site likely has multiple distinct energy users — production, HVAC, lighting, auxiliary loads. Without submetering, they're invisible in the aggregate. With it, every load becomes a lever you can pull.

Example

A farm monitors dairy shed, irrigation, and accommodation loads separately. Submetering reveals irrigation running outside solar hours. A simple schedule change reduces grid imports by 30%, with zero capital spend.

Flex:Submetering — Site Breakdown

08 — Flex:Reporting

Keep me informed with clear, ongoing performance updates.

Performance reports that actually tell you something.

Flex:Reporting delivers regular, easy-to-understand summaries of how your system is performing — financially and operationally. Reports cover energy metrics, cost savings, and system health, giving you and your stakeholders a clear, shared view of value over time. No log-ins. No data interpretation. Just a reliable update that tells you what matters.

Regular performance summaries Financial reporting System health Stakeholder communication

Commercial Value

Consistent reporting makes the value of your system visible and verifiable. It builds confidence, supports accountability, and gives you — and anyone you report to — a reliable, professional view of performance over time.

Why it matters

Value that isn't communicated is value that goes unrecognised. Flex:Reporting ensures you always know what your system is doing for you — clearly, regularly, without needing to dig for it.

Example

Your monthly report lands: $2,400 in grid cost savings, 94% solar self-consumption, two minor alerts resolved within 48 hours. A complete picture of your system's performance — with zero effort on your part.

09 — Flex:Control

Can I control my site devices directly from Flex?

From insight to action — control your devices from anywhere.

Flex:Control gives you direct, remote command over the flexible energy assets on your site — heat pumps, EV chargers, irrigation pumps, ventilation, and more. Switch devices on or off, pause or resume operation, adjust output levels, and set recurring schedules — all from within a single platform. No more fragmented OEM apps, no more manual on-site intervention, no more guessing whether a control action actually worked.

Remote device control Scheduling Control history EV charging Peak shaving Demand response

Commercial Value

Monitoring tells you what’s happening. Control lets you do something about it. Flex:Control enables peak demand reduction, demand response participation, and managed EV charging — turning flexible assets into real, measurable financial savings without ongoing manual effort.

Why it matters

Most flexible loads run on default settings or standalone OEM apps — uncoordinated with tariff windows, solar generation, or site peaks. Flex:Control consolidates device management into one place, so when the data tells you to act, you can act immediately, from anywhere.

Example

During an afternoon peak window, a site manager remotely pauses EV charging from Flex, shaving 8 kW from peak demand. A schedule kicks in at 11pm to resume overnight charging — automatically, with a full audit trail of every action taken.

Flex:Control — Device Overview
